title: CascadeGuard Actions Policy
description: Schema for .cascadeguard/actions-policy.yaml — defines which GitHub Actions are permitted in CI workflows.

default.description: Disposition for actions not matched by any allow/deny rule. 'deny' (recommended) blocks unrecognised actions; 'allow' permits them.

delay_window_hours.description: Grace period (hours) before a newly-introduced action reference triggers a violation. Set to 0 to enforce immediately. Useful with bot-authored PRs that introduce new dependencies.

allowed_owners.description: GitHub organisation or user names whose actions are trusted by default. Any action published under a listed owner is allowed regardless of the 'default' setting. Example: ['actions', 'aws-actions', 'docker'].

allowed_actions.description: Specific action references that are explicitly allowed (owner/repo). Takes precedence over 'denied_actions'. Example: ['actions/checkout', 'docker/login-action'].

denied_actions.description: Specific action references that are explicitly denied (owner/repo), regardless of other rules. Use to block known-bad or deprecated actions.

reason.description: Human-readable justification for the exception. Required for audit trail.

expires.description: Optional ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which this exception is no longer valid.

exceptions.description: One-off exceptions that override the default policy for a specific action. Each exception requires a documented reason.
